mechanism_narrative: Persistent elevation of the unemployment rate above its neutral level reduces household income expectations and consumer spending, depressing corporate earnings and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact: Historical negative correlation between UNRATE levels and forward SPX returns in post-1990 data.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year window, or 20-day returns during elevated UNRATE periods (>5%) are not lower than during low UNRATE periods (<4%) with statistical significance (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a threshold of 4.0%, it signals that the central bank is actively tightening monetary conditions, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact: Historical instances of FEDFUNDS crossing 4% upward (e.g., 2004-2006, 2018) were followed by short-term equity declines.
failure_condition: After threshold triggers, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in GDP growth and retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. Both series must be rising relative to their respective recent trends.
evidence_refs: fact: Joint regime of rising GDP and retail sales historically coincides with positive equity performance over 3-6 month horizons.
failure_condition: In periods when both GDPC1 growth (q/q annualized > 2%) and RSAFS growth (y/y > 3%) are positive, forward 60-day SPX returns are negative or rank IC of the combined signal is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession recovery regime, rising industrial production directly boosts corporate earnings and economic confidence, creating a positive tailwind for the S&P 500 over short horizons. The effect is strongest when production is rebounding from a trough.
evidence_refs: fact: In post-2009 and post-2020 recoveries, INDPRO increases were associated with positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: During NBER-defined recession troughs and subsequent 12 months, rising INDPRO (positive monthly change) does not yield positive average 5-day SPX returns, or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts activity reflects healthy construction sector, consumer confidence, and housing wealth effects, which boost aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact: Housing starts are a leading economic indicator; high levels have historically preceded positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank IC of HOUST level (or 6-month change) with forward 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year period, or the top quartile of HOUST readings does not produce higher average returns than the bottom quartile (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals imminent labor market weakness and recession risk, causing investors to discount lower future earnings and driving S&P 500 prices down over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact: Historical recessions were preceded by UNRATE increases of similar magnitude (e.g., 2001, 2008).
failure_condition: After trigger events, 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average cumulative return after triggers is not significantly negative (p>0.10) compared to non-trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated and retail sales growth decelerates, it indicates that monetary tightening is successfully cooling demand, raising recession fears and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The joint signal amplifies the individual effects.
evidence_refs: fact: Historical episodes of tightening monetary policy concurrent with slowing retail sales (e.g., 2000, 2007) preceded sharp equity declines.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 4% and RSAFS month-over-month growth is negative, the average 5-day SPX return is not negative, or the combined signal does not outperform a simple single-series threshold model in terms of directional accuracy (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion regime, a rising unemployment rate is a particularly strong negative signal because it indicates that the labor market is turning before earnings peak, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as investors price in a downturn.
evidence_refs: fact: In late expansion phases (e.g., 2007-2008), rising UNRATE preceded market declines.
failure_condition: During periods identified as late-cycle by the NBER or yield curve inversion, the rank IC of 3-month change in UNRATE with forward 20-day SPX returns is not negative, or the average return after UNRATE increases is not significantly different from zero.
